page: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. The server is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the D16pls_v6 demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score and strong results in large-block read and write memory bandwidth tests, reaching 310.02 GB/sec and 124.68 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is also strong at 33.11 GB/sec. Other computational benchmarks, including stress-ng CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core operations, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average performance tier. Large LLM inference workloads show limitations, with weak prompt processing speeds on 7B parameter models.

The D16pls_v6 offers a resource-dense, compute-focused profile with a lower memory-to-CPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated physical cores but do not demand massive memory footprints. Since local storage is absent, it relies on external premium storage options. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated ARM architecture.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, this server provides a compute-focused 2.0 GB per core ratio without bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory performance, highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score and high large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average CPU and database performance. The absence of local storage requires external premium storage integration. This configuration represents a cost-efficient option for compute-intensive workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and high memory bandwidth, optimized for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is a 16-vCPU ARM virtual machine with 32 GB RAM, offering strong memory bandwidth for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier memory performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth
